title: 🚌 PSV Driver Training
description: Professional training for matatu, bus, and taxi drivers focusing on passenger safety, commercial regulations, and customer service excellence.

units.title: PSV Licensing & NTSA Regulations
units.content:
# PSV Licensing & NTSA Regulations

## Overview
This unit covers the legal requirements for operating a Public Service Vehicle in Kenya.

## Key Requirements
- PSV badge application and renewal process
- Speed governor compliance (80 km/h maximum)
- Annual NTSA vehicle inspection
- Road service license requirements
- Insurance coverage for PSV operations

## PSV Badge
All PSV drivers must obtain a PSV badge from NTSA. Requirements include:
- Valid driving license
- Certificate of Good Conduct
- Medical examination
- PSV training completion

## Speed Governors
- All PSV vehicles must have functional speed governors
- Maximum speed limited to 80 km/h
- Tampering with speed governors is illegal
- Regular calibration required

## Vehicle Inspection
Annual inspection covers:
- Brakes and suspension
- Lights and signals
- Tyres and wheels
- Speed governor functionality
- Emergency exits and equipment
key_points: PSV badge required for all commercial passenger drivers
Valid insurance and road service license required
Tampering with speed governors is a criminal offense

units.title: Passenger Safety & Rights
units.content:
# Passenger Safety & Rights

## Passenger Rights
- Right to a safe journey
- Right to be informed of fares
- Right to receive a receipt
- Right to respectful treatment
- Right to report complaints

## Safe Boarding Procedures
1. Stop only at designated stages
2. Allow passengers to alight first
3. Assist elderly and disabled passengers
4. Ensure passengers are seated before moving
5. Count passengers to prevent overcrowding

## Emergency Evacuation
- Identify emergency exits
- Remain calm and give clear instructions
- Use emergency hammers for windows
- Assist passengers with mobility issues
- Account for all passengers after evacuation

## Lost Property
- Secure lost items immediately
- Log items with description and location
- Store in designated lost property area
- Attempt to contact owner if identified
- Transfer to police station after 30 days
key_points: Passengers have right to safe, respectful service

units.title: Route Planning & Time Management
units.content:
# Route Planning & Time Management

## Route Optimization
- Study traffic patterns for different times
- Identify alternative routes for diversions
- Know peak hour congestion areas
- Plan for construction zones
- Use navigation apps for real-time updates

## Time Management
- Start trips on schedule
- Build in buffer time for delays
- Take mandatory rest breaks
- Log driving hours accurately
- Maximum 8 hours driving per day

## Fuel Efficiency
- Smooth acceleration and braking
- Maintain steady speed
- Avoid unnecessary idling
- Check tyre pressure regularly
- Plan routes to minimize backtracking

## Documentation
- Maintain trip log books
- Record departure and arrival times
- Note any incidents or delays
- Track fuel consumption
- Report mechanical issues immediately
key_points: Study traffic patterns and plan alternative routes
Build buffer time for unexpected delays

units.title: Customer Service Excellence
units.content:
# Customer Service Excellence

## Professional Conduct
- Wear clean, professional uniform
- Greet passengers warmly
- Speak respectfully at all times
- Be patient and helpful
- Maintain vehicle cleanliness

## Communication Skills
- Speak clearly and audibly
- Announce upcoming stops
- Explain fare structure clearly
- Listen to passenger concerns
- Use appropriate language

## Handling Difficult Situations
- Stay calm and professional
- Listen without interrupting
- Acknowledge the issue
- Offer reasonable solutions
- Escalate if necessary

## Fare Disputes
- Display fare chart prominently
- Explain charges clearly
- Issue receipts for all fares
- Keep change readily available
- Refer serious disputes to supervisor

## Building Customer Loyalty
- Remember regular passengers
- Provide consistent service
- Go the extra mile when possible
- Handle complaints professionally
- Create positive passenger experience
key_points: Stay calm when handling difficult passengers

units.title: Defensive Driving for PSV
units.content:
# Defensive Driving for PSV

## Core Principles
- Anticipate hazards before they develop
- Maintain 360-degree awareness
- Keep safe following distance (4 seconds minimum)
- Scan 12-15 seconds ahead
- Prepare for others' mistakes

## Hazard Anticipation
- Watch for passengers waiting to board
- Anticipate sudden stops
- Be alert for other drivers' blind spots
- Watch for pedestrians near stages
- Expect the unexpected from other road users

## Managing Blind Spots
- Adjust mirrors for maximum visibility
- Check blind spots before lane changes
- Be aware of larger vehicle limitations
- Use convex mirrors for wider view
- Turn head to check critical areas

## Emergency Braking
- Apply firm, steady pressure
- Avoid locking wheels
- Use engine braking on descents
- Maintain steering control
- Warn passengers before hard braking

## Weather Conditions
- Reduce speed in rain (50 km/h max)
- Increase following distance in wet conditions
- Use headlights in low visibility
- Avoid standing water
- Be extra cautious during first rain after dry spell
key_points: Check blind spots before any lane change
Reduce speed significantly in wet conditions

units.title: Fare Management & Ticketing
units.content:
# Fare Management & Ticketing

## Fare Collection
- Know fare stages and prices
- Display fare chart visibly
- Announce upcoming fare stages
- Handle cash securely
- Give correct change promptly

## Digital Payments
- Accept M-Pesa payments
- Verify payment confirmations
- Use tap-to-pay where available
- Have backup for system outages
- Train on payment device operation

## Ticketing Systems
- Issue tickets for all fares
- Record ticket numbers
- Validate tickets before boarding
- Keep ticket stock secure
- Report lost or stolen tickets immediately

## Revenue Reconciliation
- Count cash at end of shift
- Match tickets to cash collected
- Report discrepancies immediately
- Secure cash during transport
- Deposit daily revenue

## Fraud Prevention
- Never accept damaged notes
- Be aware of counterfeit money
- Keep cash drawer closed
- Don't leave valuables visible
- Report suspicious activity
key_points: Display fare chart prominently in vehicle

units.title: Vehicle Inspection for PSV
units.content:
# Vehicle Inspection for PSV

## Daily Pre-Trip Inspection
- Check tyre pressure and tread depth
- Verify all lights and signals work
- Test brakes before departure
- Check fluid levels (oil, coolant, brake)
- Inspect mirrors for cracks and positioning

## Interior Inspection
- Verify seat belts functional
- Check emergency exits
- Test horn and wipers
- Ensure fire extinguisher present
- Verify first aid kit complete

## Speed Governor Check
- Confirm speed governor is sealed
- Test governor operation
- Check calibration sticker valid
- Report tampering immediately
- Document governor readings

## Defect Reporting
- Log all defects found
- Report serious issues immediately
- Do not drive with safety defects
- Follow repair procedures
- Verify repairs before next trip

## Documentation
- Complete daily inspection sheet
- Record odometer readings
- Log fuel and fluid top-ups
- Document any incidents
- Keep records for NTSA inspection
key_points: Complete daily pre-trip inspection before driving
Check tyres, lights, brakes every day
Verify speed governor is functional and sealed
